skill.name: api-endpoint-creation
skill.description: Next.js 15+ API endpoint creation patterns with Supabase and workspace validation
skill.summary: Build workspace-scoped Next.js API routes with Supabase access checks and standard response helpers.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: The static shell execution, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance findings are false positives from Markdown code fences and API route terminology in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration behavior was found. Two low-severity guidance issues remain because the examples return raw backend error messages and show minimal request body validation.

low_findings.title: Backend Error Message Exposure in Examples
low_findings.description: The GET and POST examples return error.message directly to API callers. This is useful during development but can reveal database details in production APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly pass error.message into errorResponse. The issue is limited to guidance code and does not show malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Minimal Request Body Validation in POST Example
low_findings.description: The POST example parses req.json and only checks for a name value before inserting user-provided data. Real endpoints should validate shape, types, and allowed fields.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code shows direct body parsing and insertion after a single required-field check. The pattern is common in examples, so the impact depends on implementation context.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Dismissed as Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution findings correspond to TypeScript Markdown code fences. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings have no supporting malicious or executable context in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown examples and checklist text, not executable shell, cryptographic, or reconnaissance logic. No evidence found for malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Direct Error Message Returned to Caller
dangerous_patterns.description: Example handlers return backend error.message values through API responses. Production implementations should use generic client messages and server-side logging.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly visible in both example handlers. It is a low-severity disclosure risk in generated endpoint code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Loose Body Validation Before Insert
dangerous_patterns.description: The POST pattern accepts parsed request data after checking only one field. Implementations should enforce schemas and reject unexpected fields.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The example shows limited validation before insertion. It is guidance risk rather than confirmed exploitable code.

content.value_statement: Teams often repeat fragile API route setup across Next.js services. This skill provides a standard Supabase pattern for workspace checks, scoped queries, and consistent responses.
actual_capabilities: Shows a standard GET route pattern for Next.js 15 API endpoints.
Shows a POST route pattern with workspace validation and created_by tracking.
Identifies required helper imports for Supabase and API response handling.
Emphasizes workspace_id filtering for multi-tenant data access.
Provides a checklist for consistent API route implementation.
limitations: Does not include full schema validation for request bodies.
Does not provide complete CRUD patterns for every HTTP method.
Assumes existing project helpers such as validateUserAndWorkspace and errorResponse.
Does not include automated tests or database policy examples.

use_cases.title: Add a Workspace-Scoped GET Route
use_cases.description: Create an API route that validates workspace access before querying Supabase data.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Endpoint Patterns
use_cases.description: Give contributors a common structure for imports, validation, database access, and responses.

use_cases.title: Review Multi-Tenant API Safety
use_cases.description: Check that new endpoints require workspace_id and filter database queries by workspace.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic GET Endpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new read endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-endpoint-creation skill to draft a Next.js GET endpoint for my table. Include workspace validation and a Supabase workspace_id filter.

prompt_templates.title: Create a POST Endpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: Add a write endpoint with ownership fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill pattern to create a POST endpoint for a workspace resource. Include user validation, created_by tracking, and consistent success and error responses.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor an Existing Route
prompt_templates.scenario: Bring an older route into the project standard
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this API route against the api-endpoint-creation checklist. Update it to validate workspace access and filter all Supabase queries by workspace_id.

prompt_templates.title: Harden a Production Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the skill pattern, then improve it for production by adding request schema validation, generic client errors, and notes for server-side logging.

output_examples.input: Create a route that lists projects for one workspace.
output_examples.output: A Next.js GET route outline with required imports, workspaceId extraction, access validation, Supabase query scoping, and a success response.

output_examples.input: Add a route that creates a client record.
output_examples.output: A POST route outline that validates workspace access, reads request data, records the creating user, inserts a workspace-scoped row, and returns a created response.

output_examples.input: Review this endpoint for workspace isolation.
output: Checks that every Supabase query filters by workspace_id.
Flags missing response and error handling conventions.

best_practices: Add schema validation before using request body data in a database insert.
Return generic client errors and log detailed backend errors server-side.
Keep workspace_id filtering mandatory for every tenant-owned table query.
anti_patterns: Do not trust workspace_id without checking user access to that workspace.
Do not query tenant-owned data without a workspace_id filter.
Do not copy example placeholders without replacing table names and types.

faq.question: What framework does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Next.js 15+ API routes using TypeScript.

faq.question: Does it require Supabase?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples assume a server-side Supabase client and project-specific helper functions.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is marked as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it generate complete production-ready endpoints?
faq.answer: It provides a strong starting pattern, but production code should add schema validation and safer error handling.

faq.question: What security control is most important here?
faq.answer: Every route should validate workspace access and filter tenant data by workspace_id.

faq.question: Does it include automated tests?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not include test templates or generated test files.
